@ovofaithh @cz_binance your token has been deployed! the ca is 0x462772Bf4C512de42ec3ce9B8d6e66cd2692bBa3\n\nview it on bankr: https://t.co/Id0CXdcygD https://t.co/fLHWE2afXS

is the decentralized "git for ai agents" protocol on base. it's building the foundation layer for autonomous systems to manage, fork, and version code on-chain.

the "alpha drop" refers to two major catalysts:
• xai program acceptance: has been admitted into the (elon musk's ai company) partner program. this aligns them with the grok ecosystem and provides a direct pipeline for agentic development.
• x402 agent marketplace: is launching a marketplace using the x402 protocol (http 402 "payment required"). this allows ai agents to autonomously discover and pay for services (like gitlawb's infrastructure) without human intervention.

$GITLAWB token stats (base):
• contract: 0x5f980dcfc4c0fa3911554cf5ab288ed0eb13dba3
• market cap: ~$2.76m
• 24h volume: ~$964k
• sentiment: extremely bullish. the project's openclaude (a fork of anthropic's claude code) has already crossed 20,000 github stars.
• tokenomics: the team has announced a plan to use protocol revenue to buy back and burn 90% of the current supply.

the narrative is shifting from "ai memes" to "ai infrastructure," and is currently the flag-bearer for decentralized devops for agents.

@bc1beat ������

That’s it.

No API keys.

No subscriptions.

No manual billing.

Just **pay-per-request infrastructure for agents.**

This is what agent-native commerce actually looks like.
What actually happens when an agent makes a payment via x402? \n\nNot magic. Just clean infrastructure. https://t.co/LsjqJK9gYc

1/ Agent requests a resource (API, data, compute)

2/ Server responds with **HTTP 402 Payment Required**

3/ Agent evaluates → decides to pay → sends payment to Server

4/ Payment is executed (e.g. USDC) via an x402 facilitator

5/ Server unlocks and returns the resource
